schema.title: Response Send Otp Auth Send Otp Post
Response List Tiers Billing Tiers Get
Response Stripe Webhook Billing Webhook Post
Response Delete Project Projects  Project Id  Delete

put.description:
Replace the project's schema with a new version.

When ``extraction_mode`` is included and differs from the project's
current mode, the project's mode is flipped and every Document's
``DocumentExtraction`` rows are deleted and recreated to match. This
is the canonical way to lock in the mode the user picked at draft-
from-sample time.

post.description:
Generate a schema draft from a natural-language description.

The draft is NOT saved — the frontend is expected to let the user edit it
and then save via ``PUT /projects/{id}/schema``.

Presigned PUT for an ephemeral sample upload used by draft-from-sample.

The resulting R2 object is NOT registered as a Document. After the
follow-up ``draft-from-sample`` call, the sample is deleted on a best-
effort basis. The two-step flow (presign → upload → draft) avoids
streaming bytes through the backend.

Generate a schema draft from an ephemeral sample upload.

Pipeline: R2 GET (sample) → TextIn OCR (page-scoped if ``page_number``
is set) → OpenAI → validated draft. The R2 sample object is deleted
best-effort after OCR.

Pass ``extraction_mode`` to indicate whether the user is designing
a schema for whole-document extraction or per-page extraction; the
response echoes it back so the frontend can pass the same value
through to ``PUT /schema``.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by extraction status. Repeat the param to OR multiple values, e.g. ?status=pending&status=processing. A document matches if any of its extractions has the given status.
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by extraction status. Repeat the param to OR multiple values, e.g. ?status=pending&status=processing. A document matches if any of its extractions has the given status.

parameters.name: stale

schema.description: true = docs with at least one extraction whose schema_version is null or older than the project's current version (candidates for rerun-stale). false = docs whose extractions are all current. Omit for no filter.
schema.title: Stale

parameters.description: true = docs with at least one extraction whose schema_version is null or older than the project's current version (candidates for rerun-stale). false = docs whose extractions are all current. Omit for no filter.

get.description:
Preview how many documents would be rerun by ``rerun-stale``.

``estimated_pages`` charges 1 per per-page extraction and the
document's ``page_count`` per whole-doc extraction (best-effort —
docs with unknown page counts contribute 0).

schema.title: Response Count Stale Documents Projects  Project Id  Documents Stale Count Get

post.description:
Requeue every stale ``DocumentExtraction`` in the project (schema_version
null or strictly < project.schema_version). In-flight rows are skipped
silently.

schema.title: Response Rerun Stale Documents Projects  Project Id  Documents Rerun Stale Post
Response Delete Document Projects  Project Id  Documents  Document Id  Delete

get.description:
List the document's extraction units.

In ``document`` mode this returns one row (page_number null). In
``per_page`` mode it returns one row per page, ordered by page
number ascending. ``result`` is excluded from list payloads — fetch
the singular endpoint for the full TextIn response.

Single extraction with the full TextIn response embedded.

The visualizer consumes ``result.citations`` + ``result.pages`` to
render bounding boxes; clients showing only data can use the list
endpoint and skip the heavier payload.

post.description:
Requeue a document for re-extraction with the current schema.

Resets every ``DocumentExtraction`` for this document back to
``pending``. Rejects with 409 ``DOCUMENT_IN_FLIGHT`` if any
extraction is currently pending or processing — wait for the queue
to drain and try again. Each rerun spends page quota again when the
worker processes it.

get.description:
Proxy the TextIn page image by ``image_id``.

TextIn's image download endpoint requires the service credentials —
which MUST NOT reach the browser. The frontend calls us with an
authenticated request; we look up the ``image_id`` across the
document's success extractions, download from TextIn with our
credentials, and stream the bytes back.

Catalogue of available templates. Does not include the field lists —
call the single-template endpoint to get those.

schema.title: Response List Schema Templates Schema Templates Get

get.description:
List a project's already-parsed documents with their extracted data.

Results are sorted by ``parsed_time`` (= ``Document.finished_at``) DESC.
Stale documents (schema_version lags) are included with ``is_stale=true``
so the frontend can badge them rather than hide them.

parameters.name: parsed_from

schema.description: Inclusive lower bound on parsed_time (yyyy-mm-dd, UTC).
schema.title: Parsed From

parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound on parsed_time (yyyy-mm-dd, UTC).

parameters.name: parsed_to

schema.description: Inclusive upper bound on parsed_time (yyyy-mm-dd, UTC).
schema.title: Parsed To

parameters.description: Inclusive upper bound on parsed_time (yyyy-mm-dd, UTC).

post.description:
Queue an async export. Returns immediately; poll the list/get endpoints
until ``status`` is ``success``, then hit ``/download``.

409 if the user already has the max allowed concurrent jobs queued.

schema.title: Response Delete Export Job Projects  Project Id  Parsed Data Exports  Job Id  Delete

get.description:
Return a short-lived R2 presigned URL for the generated file.

Not a 302 redirect because browsers can't pass our Authorization header
through a redirect. Client code fetches this endpoint, then does
``window.location = response.download_url`` (or fetches the URL
directly).

schema.title: Response Download Export Projects  Project Id  Parsed Data Exports  Job Id  Download Get

ExportFilter.title: ExportFilter
ExportFilter.description:
Optional filters applied when gathering the parsed data to export.

Matches the ``GET /parsed-data`` list endpoint semantics: inclusive date
bounds on ``Document.finished_at``. Null bounds mean "unbounded".

ExtractionsSummary.title: ExtractionsSummary
ExtractionsSummary.description:
Per-status counts of a document's extractions.

Used in list responses so the frontend can render progress bars
("3/5 success, 1 failed, 1 pending") without a second round-trip to
/extractions.

ParsedDataItem.title: ParsedDataItem
ParsedDataItem.description:
One extraction unit's worth of already-extracted data for the list view.

In ``document`` mode each row corresponds to one Document
(``page_number`` is null). In ``per_page`` mode each row is one page
of one Document — the same ``document_id`` repeats across rows that
differ only in ``page_number``.

``data`` is the raw ``extracted_schema`` dict from TextIn — the frontend
uses the project's current schema to decide how to render each field
(primitive / object / array with fold-to-count badge).

SchemaDraftFromPromptRequest.title: SchemaDraftFromPromptRequest
SchemaDraftFromPromptRequest.description: Natural-language description of the fields the user wants extracted.

SchemaDraftFromSampleRequest.title: SchemaDraftFromSampleRequest
SchemaDraftFromSampleRequest.description:
Generate a schema draft from an ephemeral sample upload.

``page_number`` is required when ``extraction_mode`` is ``per_page`` —
we OCR just that one page so the LLM doesn't average across multiple
records. Omit ``page_number`` for whole-document samples.

``prompt`` lets the user steer the LLM ("only headline fields", etc.).

SchemaDraftResponse.title: SchemaDraftResponse
SchemaDraftResponse.description:
Draft schema returned by the AI endpoints. Not persisted; the frontend
is expected to POST these fields back via ``PUT /projects/{id}/schema``
after the user confirms them.

``chosen_extraction_mode`` echoes back the mode the draft was made for
(whole-doc OCR vs single-page OCR). The frontend should pass this
through as ``extraction_mode`` on the subsequent ``PUT /schema`` so the
project's mode matches what the schema was designed for.

SchemaSampleUploadRequest.title: SchemaSampleUploadRequest
SchemaSampleUploadRequest.description:
Mirrors :class:`PresignUploadRequest` but bound to schema-draft sample
uploads. The resulting R2 object is intentionally NOT registered as a
Document — it's OCR'd once during ``draft_from_sample`` and deleted
best-effort after.

SchemaTemplateRead.title: SchemaTemplateRead
SchemaTemplateRead.description: Returned by the single-template endpoint — includes the full fields.

SchemaTemplateSummary.title: SchemaTemplateSummary
SchemaTemplateSummary.description: Returned by the list endpoint — no fields, just the metadata.
